symptoms: runny nose, sneezing, sore throat, cough, congestion, mild fever, headache, fatigue

symptoms: high fever, chills, severe body aches, muscle pain, headache, fatigue, cough, sore throat, runny nose
description: Influenza is a contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses. It can cause mild to severe illness and can lead to complications in high-risk individuals.

medications: Paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever and pain|Decongestants for nasal symptoms|Antiviral medications if prescribed early
followup_questions: When did symptoms start suddenly?|Do you have severe body pain?|Have you been vaccinated this year?|Are you in a high-risk group such as elderly or pregnant?

symptoms: fever, dry cough, fatigue, loss of taste, loss of smell, shortness of breath, body aches, headache, sore throat, congestion
description: COVID-19 is an infectious disease caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus. Symptoms range from mild to severe and can include respiratory complications requiring hospitalization.

medications: Paracetamol for fever|Vitamins D and Zinc|Consult doctor for antiviral treatment|Oxygen support if needed

symptoms: high fever, productive cough with phlegm, chest pain, shortness of breath, rapid breathing, fatigue, chills, sweating
description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs. The air sacs may fill with fluid or pus, causing cough with phlegm, fever, chills, and difficulty breathing.
precautions: Seek medical attention promptly|Complete prescribed antibiotic course|Rest and avoid strenuous activity|Stay well hydrated|Avoid smoking
medications: Antibiotics for bacterial pneumonia (prescription needed)|Paracetamol for fever|Cough suppressants|Bronchodilators if needed
followup_questions: Do you have sharp chest pain when breathing?|Are you coughing up colored phlegm?|How high is your fever?|Do you have difficulty taking deep breaths?

symptoms: persistent cough lasting weeks, coughing blood, night sweats, unexplained weight loss, fatigue, fever, chest pain
description: Tuberculosis is a serious infectious disease that mainly affects the lungs. It is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis and spreads through airborne droplets when infected people cough or sneeze.
precautions: See a doctor immediately|Complete full TB treatment course|Isolate during infectious period|Use proper ventilation|Wear mask
medications: Anti-TB medications (strict prescription required)|Vitamins B6 supplements during treatment|Nutritional support
followup_questions: How long have you had the cough?|Have you noticed blood in your sputum?|Have you experienced unexplained weight loss?|Have you been in contact with a TB patient?

symptoms: wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness, coughing especially at night, difficulty breathing during exercise
description: Asthma is a chronic condition affecting the airways. Airways become inflamed and narrowed, causing breathing difficulties. Triggers include allergens, exercise, cold air, and stress.
precautions: Avoid known triggers|Keep rescue inhaler accessible|Monitor peak flow regularly|Follow asthma action plan|Avoid smoking and secondhand smoke
medications: Short-acting bronchodilator inhaler (Salbutamol)|Corticosteroid inhaler for prevention|Antihistamines if allergic trigger|Consult doctor for long-term management

symptoms: frequent urination, excessive thirst, unexplained weight loss, fatigue, blurred vision, slow-healing wounds, frequent infections
description: Diabetes is a chronic condition where the body cannot properly produce or use insulin. Type 1 involves no insulin production; Type 2 involves insulin resistance. Both cause high blood sugar levels.
precautions: Monitor blood glucose regularly|Follow diabetic diet|Exercise regularly|Take medications as prescribed|Get regular eye and foot checkups
medications: Metformin for Type 2 (prescription)|Insulin therapy as prescribed|Blood glucose monitoring supplies|Vitamins and supplements as recommended
followup_questions: What is your current blood sugar level?|Do you have a family history of diabetes?|When did you last eat?|Do you have any wounds that are not healing?

symptoms: headache especially morning, dizziness, blurred vision, chest pain, shortness of breath, nosebleeds, irregular heartbeat
description: Hypertension or high blood pressure is a condition where the force of blood against artery walls is consistently too high. Often called the silent killer as it may have no symptoms for years.
precautions: Reduce salt intake|Exercise regularly|Maintain healthy weight|Limit alcohol and caffeine|Monitor blood pressure regularly|Manage stress
medications: Antihypertensive medications (prescription required)|Potassium-rich foods|Omega-3 supplements|Reduce caffeine consumption
followup_questions: What is your current blood pressure reading?|Do you have a family history of hypertension?|Do you smoke or consume alcohol regularly?|What is your stress level like?

symptoms: nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, stomach cramps, abdominal pain, fever, headache, muscle aches
description: Gastroenteritis is inflammation of the stomach and intestines, usually caused by viral or bacterial infection. It commonly leads to vomiting and diarrhea and can cause dehydration.
precautions: Stay hydrated with clear fluids and ORS|Rest the stomach with bland foods|Wash hands frequently|Avoid dairy and fatty foods temporarily|Seek help if dehydration is severe

symptoms: severe throbbing headache, sensitivity to light, sensitivity to sound, nausea, vomiting, aura or visual disturbances, one-sided head pain
description: Migraine is a neurological condition characterized by intense, debilitating headaches often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and extreme sensitivity to light and sound. Episodes can last hours to days.
precautions: Rest in a dark quiet room|Stay hydrated|Identify and avoid triggers|Use cold or warm compress|Manage stress and sleep schedule

symptoms: high fever, severe headache, pain behind eyes, joint and muscle pain, rash, mild bleeding from gums or nose, fatigue, nausea
description: Dengue fever is a mosquito-borne viral disease. Severe dengue can be life-threatening. It is prevalent in tropical and subtropical climates worldwide, particularly in urban areas.
precautions: Seek medical attention promptly|Use mosquito repellent|Rest and stay hydrated|Monitor platelet count|Avoid NSAIDs like aspirin
medications: Paracetamol for fever (avoid ibuprofen and aspirin)|ORS for hydration|Platelet transfusion if needed (hospital)|Regular blood monitoring

symptoms: cyclical fever and chills, sweating, headache, nausea, vomiting, muscle pain, fatigue, anemia symptoms

symptoms: burning sensation during urination, frequent urge to urinate, cloudy or foul-smelling urine, pelvic pain, low-grade fever, blood in urine

symptoms: skin rash, redness, itching, swelling, hives, dry skin, blisters, eczema patches, contact dermatitis

symptoms: fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath, dizziness, cold hands and feet, chest pain, headache, brittle nails

symptoms: joint pain, joint stiffness, swelling around joints, reduced range of motion, redness around joints, warmth in joints, fatigue
